title: University of Cambridge — AI Policy Tracker Report (Contract V2)
summary: Cambridge broadly permits appropriate use of GenAI tools but has no single university-wide AI policy. Instead it provides an AI Policy Framework for local determination by triposes, departments, faculties, and colleges. 6 verified sources yielded 10 approved findings covering data input restrictions, licensed tool mandates (Copilot/Gemini/NotebookLM), human review requirements, academic misconduct rules, AI detection software unreliability, examiner restrictions, and general principles. 2 claims (purpose limitation + automated decision-making, examiner exception clause) need evidence amendments. 10 source candidates were rejected (research showcases, 404s, redirects, no-AI-content pages).
limitations: claim-cam-info-compliance-5 (needs_changes): Purpose limitation claim scope exceeds evidence — automated decision-making sentence present in source but not captured in evidence snippet. Requires additional evidence candidate or claim amendment.

claim-cam-assessment-3 (needs_changes): Examiner restrictions claim scope exceeds evidence — exception clause (examiners may use tools for own writing) present in source but not captured in evidence snippet. Requires additional evidence candidate or claim amendment.

This report is not legal advice. Descriptions of university data protection, procurement, or security guidance do not constitute legal interpretation of GDPR, UK data protection law, or any other regulation.

This report is not academic integrity advice. Descriptions of academic misconduct policies are factual summaries of institutional positions, not guidance on how students or staff should handle integrity questions.
This report does not constitute an official university statement. The tracker synthesises publicly available policy information from official university sources for informational purposes only.
Source pages may change after the snapshot date (2026-05-05). Always verify against the original source URL for the most current version.
The AI Policy Framework is a downloadable .docx template. The tracker summarises the framework page description; local implementations may vary by department, faculty, or college.
sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
